stories.title: Built anomaly detection system that saved $500K
stories.situation: Our procurement team was manually reviewing thousands of invoices monthly, missing pricing anomalies that were costing the company significant money.
stories.task: I was asked to find a way to automatically detect anomalous pricing in our vendor invoices to prevent overpayment.
stories.action: I built an XGBoost-based anomaly detection model using historical invoice data. Engineered features from vendor patterns, seasonal pricing, and contract terms. Used SHAP values to make the model explainable for the procurement team.
stories.result: The system identified $500K in overpayments in its first quarter. Reduced manual review time by 70%. The model is now running in production with 92% precision.
applicableQuestions: Tell me about a time you used data to drive business impact
Describe a project where you built something from scratch
How do you make ML models explainable to non-technical stakeholders?

stories.title: Dashboard that served 200+ users and reduced ad-hoc requests 35%
stories.situation: Stakeholders across 5 departments were constantly requesting one-off reports, consuming 40% of our team's bandwidth.
stories.task: Create a self-service analytics solution that would empower business users to answer their own questions.
stories.action: Designed and built a Tableau dashboard suite with 12 interactive views. Conducted user research with key stakeholders. Implemented row-level security for data access control. Ran training sessions for 200+ users.
stories.result: Ad-hoc reporting requests dropped 35%. User adoption reached 200+ active monthly users. Freed up team bandwidth to focus on strategic ML projects.
applicableQuestions: Tell me about a time you built something that scaled
How do you handle competing priorities from multiple stakeholders?
